Enable TLS for the running CockroachDB cluster in the cockroachdb namespace.

The cluster is currently running in insecure mode. An openssl-toolbox pod
is available for certificate generation.

GOAL:
1. Insecure access is rejected:
   ```bash
   kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- \
     ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT 1;"
   ```
2. Secure access succeeds:
   ```bash
   kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- \
     ./cockroach sql --certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs -e "SELECT 1;"
   ```

codex
The cluster has a valid-looking cert secret already, but the pods do not mount it and the probes are still plain HTTP. I’m going to verify the certificate subjects/SANs, then patch the StatefulSet to mount the cert directory and start CockroachDB without `--insecure`.

codex
I found the secret has the right CockroachDB file names, a `CN=node` node cert, a `CN=root` client cert, localhost SANs, service SANs, and pod SANs. I’m patching the StatefulSet now: add the secret mount with restrictive key permissions, switch the start command to `--certs-dir`, and make health probes use HTTPS.
A usable CockroachDB cert secret already exists, so no replacement generation was needed.

  ✓ Generate and install TLS certificates using the openssl toolbox

codex
I’m using the existing cert secret rather than regenerating it because it is valid through June 22, 2027 and already covers the pod/service names the cluster uses.

codex
The first patch attempt was rejected before it changed anything because of a JSON quoting issue. I’m reissuing the same patch with a literal JSON payload so the API server sees exactly one object.
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "kubectl -n cockroachdb patch statefulset crdb-cluster --type merge -p '
{
  \"spec\": {
    \"template\": {
      \"spec\": {
        \"containers\": [
          {
            \"name\": \"db\",
            \"command\": [
              \"/bin/bash\",
              \"-c\",
              \"exec /cockroach/cockroach start --logtostderr=INFO --certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs --advertise-host="'$(POD_NAME).crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080 --port=26257 --cache=25% --max-sql-memory=25% --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257"
            ],
            "livenessProbe": {
              "httpGet": {
                "path": "/health",
                "port": "http",
                "scheme": "HTTPS"
              }
            },
            "readinessProbe": {
              "httpGet": {
                "path": "/health?ready=1",
                "port": "http",
                "scheme": "HTTPS"
              }
            },
            "volumeMounts": [
              {
                "name": "certs",
                "mountPath": "/cockroach/cockroach-certs",
                "readOnly": true
              }
            ]
          }
        ],
        "volumes": [
          {
            "name": "certs",
            "secret": {
              "secretName": "crdb-certs",
              "defaultMode": 256
            }
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}

codex
The patch rendered with only the cert mount, so I’m correcting that immediately by restoring the `datadir` mount alongside the cert mount. The StatefulSet still has the PVC template; the pod template just needs the mount back.
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "kubectl -n cockroachdb patch statefulset crdb-cluster --type merge -p '
{
  \"spec\": {
    \"template\": {
      \"spec\": {
        \"containers\": [
          {
            \"name\": \"db\",
            \"volumeMounts\": [
              {
                \"name\": \"datadir\",
                \"mountPath\": \"/cockroach/cockroach-data\"
              },
              {
                \"name\": \"certs\",
                \"mountPath\": \"/cockroach/cockroach-certs\",
                \"readOnly\": true
              }
            ]
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}

codex
The second JSON merge patch replaced the container array rather than merging it, which removed fields like `image` from the desired template. The running pods are still on the old revision, and I’m restoring the full container spec with the secure command now.
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "kubectl -n cockroachdb patch statefulset crdb-cluster --type merge -p '
{
  \"spec\": {
    \"template\": {
      \"spec\": {
        \"containers\": [
          {
            \"name\": \"db\",
            \"image\": \"cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0\",
            \"imagePullPolicy\": \"IfNotPresent\",
            \"command\": [
              \"/bin/bash\",
              \"-c\",
              \"exec /cockroach/cockroach start --logtostderr=INFO --certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs --advertise-host="'$(POD_NAME).crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080 --port=26257 --cache=25% --max-sql-memory=25% --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257"
            ],
            "env": [
              {
                "name": "POD_NAME",
                "valueFrom": {
                  "fieldRef": {
                    "apiVersion": "v1",
                    "fieldPath": "metadata.name"
                  }
                }
              },
              {
                "name": "POD_NAMESPACE",
                "valueFrom": {
                  "fieldRef": {
                    "apiVersion": "v1",
                    "fieldPath": "metadata.namespace"
                  }
                }
              }
            ],
            "ports": [
              {
                "name": "grpc",
                "containerPort": 26257,
                "protocol": "TCP"
              },
              {
                "name": "http",
                "containerPort": 8080,
                "protocol": "TCP"
              }
            ],
            "livenessProbe": {
              "failureThreshold": 3,
              "httpGet": {
                "path": "/health",
                "port": "http",
                "scheme": "HTTPS"
              },
              "initialDelaySeconds": 30,
              "periodSeconds": 5,
              "successThreshold": 1,
              "timeoutSeconds": 1
            },
            "readinessProbe": {
              "failureThreshold": 2,
              "httpGet": {
                "path": "/health?ready=1",
                "port": "http",
                "scheme": "HTTPS"
              },
              "initialDelaySeconds": 10,
              "periodSeconds": 5,
              "successThreshold": 1,
              "timeoutSeconds": 1
            },
            "resources": {
              "limits": {
                "cpu": "1",
                "memory": "2Gi"
              },
              "requests": {
                "cpu": "1",
                "memory": "2Gi"
              }
            },
            "terminationMessagePath": "/dev/termination-log",
            "terminationMessagePolicy": "File",
            "volumeMounts": [
              {
                "name": "datadir",
                "mountPath": "/cockroach/cockroach-data"
              },
              {
                "name": "certs",
                "mountPath": "/cockroach/cockroach-certs",
                "readOnly": true
              }
            ]
          }
        ],
        "volumes": [
          {
            "name": "certs",
            "secret": {
              "secretName": "crdb-certs",
              "defaultMode": 256
            }
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}
